title: themisra.proof-of-prompt v1.0.0
description: Canonical Themisra Proof-of-Prompt attestation payload. Binds the (prompt, response, model, time) tuple of a single human-AI interaction to the user's identity, so the user can later prove cryptographically that they prompted a specific model with specific text and received a specific response at a specific moment. The on-chain attestation stores SHA-256 of the canonical JSON form of an instance of this schema; the M-of-N attestor signatures attest that the off-chain quorum service witnessed the corresponding plaintext interaction. v1 of LIP-5; sufficient for devnet and design-partner integrations. Future revisions ship as v2 with their own schema_id rather than mutating this one.

spec_version.description: Schema spec version. Pinned to a single value at this revision; future revisions ship as v2 with a new schema_id.

user_did.description: Identity of the human prompter. Either a chain-native bech32m address (`lig1...`), a W3C DID URL (`did:web:...`, `did:key:...`), or the chain-native DID form (`did:lig:<address>`). The signer of the on-chain `SubmitAttestation` tx is a SEPARATE identity (typically a relayer like Mneme acting on the user's behalf) and is recorded in the tx envelope itself.

model_id.description: Machine-readable identifier for the AI model. Recommended form: `<provider>/<model>:<version>`, e.g. `anthropic/claude-opus:4.7`, `openai/gpt-4o:2025-01-01`, `google/gemini-pro:2.5`. Free-form for v1; LIP-5 may pin a registry in a later revision.

prompt_hash.description: SHA-256 of the user's prompt text, hex-encoded (lowercase, no `0x` prefix). Canonicalization: NFC-normalized Unicode, no leading/trailing whitespace, LF line endings, UTF-8 bytes. Off-chain attestor services SHOULD reject payloads whose claimed `prompt_hash` does not match the canonicalized prompt they witnessed.

response_hash.description: SHA-256 of the AI model's response text, same canonicalization as `prompt_hash`.

created_at.description: RFC 3339 UTC timestamp of when the prompt-response interaction occurred. Off-chain attestor services SHOULD reject claimed times in the future or more than 24h in the past relative to the sign request.

system_prompt_hash.description: Optional. SHA-256 of the system prompt (if any), same canonicalization as `prompt_hash`. Distinct from `prompt_hash` because the system prompt is often configured by the tool (e.g. Mneme's wallet helper) and not authored by the user.

provider_id.description: Optional. Hostname of the inference API provider (e.g. `api.anthropic.com`, `api.openai.com`). Useful for partners filtering by provider.

tool_id.description: Optional. Identifier of the user-facing tool (e.g. `mneme`, `claude.ai/web`, `gemini-app`, `chatgpt-desktop`). One provider can host many tools; this field disambiguates.

prompt_uri.description: Optional. URI where the canonicalized prompt text can be retrieved (IPFS, HTTPS, signed S3, etc.). The chain stores only the hash; this field lets verifiers fetch the plaintext for re-hashing.

response_uri.description: Optional. URI where the canonicalized response text can be retrieved.
